patches.summary: Hardcode the openart.ai cookie domain in refreshStoredBrowserCookies so `auth refresh` actually reaches the extractor pipeline.
patches.reason: The variable was initialized to the empty string and the function immediately returned 'no cookie domain configured', leaving the rest of the function as dead code. Every user invoking `auth refresh` saw the same misleading error (greptile P1).
patches.validated_outcome: Build passes. `auth refresh` now invokes the cookie extractor against openart.ai cookies.

patches.summary: Mirror the live-request header-routing logic in --dry-run output so cookie-shaped auth is labeled Cookie, not Authorization.
patches.reason: OpenArt's cookie-based auth sets a `Cookie:` header on the live request (via looksLikeCookieString), but the dry-run printout hardcoded `Authorization:`, so developers debugging requests saw misleading output (greptile P2).
patches.validated_outcome: Dry-run output now labels the auth header to match what the request actually sends.

patches.summary: Add a first_seen_at column to the credits typed table and bucket/filter credits burn + forecast on it instead of synced_at.
patches.reason: The captured /credits/logs response shape has no per-record timestamp (validated against the manuscript's traffic-analysis credit_contract). The previous credits commands used synced_at, which is rewritten on every full resync, so credits burn --by day collapsed every historical spend into the sync-day bucket and credits forecast's 28-day window matched every record on a first sync. The new first_seen_at column captures the first observation timestamp (preserved across ON CONFLICT updates and backfilled to synced_at for legacy rows); going forward each new spend records an accurate consume-time proxy because the autorefresh path syncs on every CLI invocation (greptile P1 x2).
patches.validated_outcome: go test ./... passes. credits burn buckets by observation date, credits forecast's 28-day window only matches recently-observed spends. Migration is idempotent and backfills pre-existing rows from synced_at.

patches.summary: Add the catalog's Experimental flag to modelRow() output so models list / models show JSON exposes which models need --accept-experimental.
patches.reason: The Experimental field was set correctly on every catalog entry but modelRow() omitted it from the returned map, so callers parsing the JSON had no way to filter or warn before submitting. Discovered in the 2026-05-14 donkey-photo session: the agent picked nano-banana-pro from JSON output with no experimental signal, then learned about the opt-in flag only after the submit rejected it. Paired with SKILL.md / README.md / image gen --help updates that document the nano-banana family explicitly (regular vs Pro, no v2).
patches.validated_outcome: go test ./... passes (193 in 12 packages, including 3 new modelRow tests). go vet clean. verify_skill.py clean. `models show nano-banana-pro --agent` returns experimental=true; `models show nano-banana --agent` returns experimental=false; `image gen --help` shows both variants in examples.
